span.p:
rung 0 - static
parses? binary on PATH? args type-check?
(microseconds)

div.style: color: rgb(107, 84, 16) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
color: rgb(107, 84, 16) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
rung 2 - sandbox probe
tiny fixture, read-only, no network
(tens of ms)

div.style: color: rgb(94, 61, 18) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
every pass-k / fail-(k+1) is a
labeled example that tightens the ladder
(the RSI signal)

div.style: color: rgb(70, 88, 106) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
a failed call's REAL cost is context pollution:
a stack trace ~500-2000 tokens + a wasted recovery turn
(not wall-clock)

div.style: color: rgb(70, 88, 106) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: spend 1 us to avoid a stack trace in context
